info.title: Frontend Agent Prompt - Admin Event Entry Confirmation Fields
info.description: Implement support in the admin event UI for the new backend field event.entryConfirmationConfig. Goal: allow admins to maintain all data needed for the modern entry confirmation PDF and admission mail attachment.

get.description: Consume the new field in event list/detail loading if your client typing currently strips unknown properties.

post.description: Ensure create-event flow can submit the new nested object, even if the UI initially only exposes it on edit.

patch.summary: Existing endpoint accepts partial updates including entryConfirmationConfig
patch.description: Primary frontend task: extend the event edit form and PATCH payload mapping for the nested config object.

FrontendTask.description: Implementation brief for the frontend agent.

goal.example: Add editable event fields in Admin UI for the entry confirmation PDF configuration.

example: Use the existing event create/edit API payloads; do not invent new endpoints.
Preserve unknown existing entryConfirmationConfig fields when editing.
Allow empty optional fields and submit null or omit empty values consistently with existing form conventions.
Provide repeatable UI controls for importantNotes and scheduleItems.
Use datetime inputs for schedule item startsAt/endsAt.
Add lightweight inline help text explaining these fields feed the admission PDF/mail attachment.
Keep the UI practical and admin-oriented, not overly decorative.
Existing event edit screen loads and displays entryConfirmationConfig.
Admin can add/remove notes and schedule items.
Reload after save shows persisted values.
Empty events without the config still render without crashes.

x-agent-instructions.summary: Implement the admin UI for the new event.entryConfirmationConfig fields used by the backend entry confirmation PDF.
x-agent-instructions.preferredPlacement: Place this as a dedicated collapsible card/section inside the event edit form. If create flow already supports advanced event metadata, it may also appear there; edit form is the minimum required.
notes: Do not expose technical PDF internals or revision hashes.
Do not show legacy wording like entry_confirmation or system fallback labels.
The backend already generates the PDF automatically for accepted_open_payment mails; frontend only needs data maintenance UI.
